text: Testing plays a vital role in software development. Two common types are unit tests and integration tests. Unit tests check small parts of the code, like a single function, to ensure they work correctly. Integration tests, on the other hand, verify that different parts of the system work well together. Many developers use test-driven development (TDD), where they write tests before writing the actual code. This approach helps them think about the desired behavior first. Overall, good testing gives developers confidence that their code is reliable and that changes won't break existing features.
